sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.54 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: We say that a group$G$acts freely\/ on a group$V$if$vg v$for any nontrivial elements$g G,\; v V$. Is it true that a group$G$that can act freely on a non-trivial abelian group is embeddable in the multiplicative group of some skew-field? { V.\,D.\,Mazurov} No, it is not. For example, the group$2.A_5.2$with a quaternion Sylow 2-subgroup can act freely on an elementary abelian group of order$7^4$but ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
